_description: Design code metadata for GTM demo 2 (wall thickness sizing). Contains safety factors, formula references, and key parameters for multi-code comparison of subsea pipeline wall thickness design.

Low.description: Where failure implies low risk of human injury and minor environmental and economic consequences (e.g., water injection lines in remote locations)

Medium.description: For conditions where failure implies risk of significant environmental or economic consequences (e.g., typical oil/gas production pipeline)

High.description: For conditions where failure implies significant risk of human injury or very large environmental/economic consequences (e.g., pipeline near platform, riser)

resistance_factors._note_alpha_U: Material strength de-rating factor for normal use. alpha_U = 1.0 for supplementary requirement U.

where.t: Wall thickness (minus corrosion and fabrication tolerances)
where.f_cb: min(f_y, f_u/1.15) where f_y = SMYS * alpha_U, f_u = SMTS * alpha_U

pressure_containment_formula._note: p_li = local incidental pressure, p_e = external pressure

collapse_formula.equation: (p_c - p_el)(p_c^2 - p_p^2) = p_c * p_el * p_p * f_0 * (D/t)
collapse_formula._note: Sec 5.4.3. Requires iterative solution for p_c.

temperature_derating._note: Approximate yield strength derating for carbon steel per DNV-ST-F101 Table 5-1

codes.name: API RP 1111
codes.full_title: Design, Construction, Operation, and Maintenance of Offshore Hydrocarbon Pipelines (Limit State Design)
codes.approach: Working Stress Design (WSD) with design factors

design_factors._note: fd = design factor applied to SMYS for allowable stress. 0.72 is standard for offshore pipelines per API RP 1111.

internal_pressure_formula.equation: t_min = P_d * D / (2 * S * fd * T)
internal_pressure_formula.check: Barlow formula basis: hoop stress = P*D/(2*t) <= fd * S * T

collapse_formula._note: Simplified collapse per API RP 1111 Sec 4.3.3. External pressure <= fd * P_c.

temperature_derating._note: Per ASME B31.4 / B31.8 temperature de-rating for carbon steel

codes.name: PD 8010-2
codes.full_title: Code of Practice for Pipelines - Part 2: Subsea pipelines
codes.approach: Working Stress Design (WSD) with design factors

design_factors._note: Design factor 0.72 for hoop stress in operational condition. Based on BS tradition aligned with ASME B31.8.

where.t: Minimum wall thickness (nominal minus tolerances and corrosion allowance)

hoop_stress_formula._note: Same Barlow basis as API, but PD 8010-2 has additional equivalent stress check

equivalent_stress_check._note: Von Mises equivalent stress including hoop, axial, and shear. Per Cl. 6.4.3.

temperature_derating._note: Uses ASME B31.8 temperature de-rating factors (same as API RP 1111 values)

comparison_notes.general: DNV-ST-F101 uses LRFD (partial safety factors on loads and resistance separately), while API RP 1111 and PD 8010-2 use WSD (single design factor). DNV approach is more nuanced but more complex.
comparison_notes.conservatism_ranking: DNV High Safety Class > PD 8010-2 > DNV Medium SC ≈ API RP 1111 > DNV Low SC (generally, but depends on specific load case and D/t ratio)
comparison_notes.typical_differences: Wall thickness from DNV Medium SC is typically 0-5% thicker than API/PD 8010-2 for pressure containment. Collapse checks can drive larger differences for deepwater (high D/t) designs.
